findings.reachability: Authenticated admin (activate_plugins capability) via wp_ajax_ hook
findings.justification: After successfully dispatching the payload via sendPayloadToWpdesk(), handleAjaxRequest() returns without calling wp_die() or exit, causing WordPress to append a spurious '-1' to the AJAX response; while not a direct security vulnerability, this deviates from the required WordPress AJAX contract and can cause clients to misinterpret the response as an error.

detection_pattern.description: Finds wp_ajax_ handler registrations; the anti-pattern checks for a terminating call (wp_die or wp_send_json which internally calls wp_die). Handlers missing both may leak unexpected output or mishandle responses.

findings.reachability: Authenticated admin (activate_plugins capability + valid nonce) via POST/GET to wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
findings.justification: The values $_REQUEST['reason'] (line 49) and $_REQUEST['additional_info'] (line 55) are copied verbatim into the payload array and forwarded in a JSON body to https://data.wpdesk.org/ via wp_remote_post() without any sanitization or length-capping; an admin-level attacker could inject arbitrary strings (including oversized values) into the vendor's telemetry endpoint, constituting telemetry-data injection against the third-party collector.

detection_pattern.section_name: Unsanitized $_REQUEST forwarded to external HTTP request
detection_pattern.description: Matches direct $_REQUEST array reads; the anti-pattern checks for sanitization wrappers. Files matching the pattern but not the anti-pattern may be passing raw user input to wp_remote_post/get or other external sinks.

findings.reachability: Authenticated admin (activate_plugins capability) via any HTTP method (GET, POST, or COOKIE collision)
findings.justification: The handler reads from $_REQUEST instead of $_POST; $_REQUEST merges GET, POST, and COOKIE superglobals, meaning a crafted GET request carrying a stolen or predictable nonce token could trigger the handler via a URL alone (e.g. embedded in an img src or bookmark), slightly widening the CSRF surface even when a nonce is present.

detection_pattern.description: Finds AJAX handler functions; the anti-pattern checks that input is read from $_POST rather than $_REQUEST. Handlers using $_REQUEST accept GET-parameter input which can be triggered cross-origin via URL navigation, reducing the effectiveness of nonce-based CSRF protection.
